travelers.luggage: 3 small suitcases + small bags, possibly 1 large suitcase on return

tripDetails.terrain: Will drive on gravel/ripio roads — need SUV with ground clearance

vehicleRecommendation.whySUV: Ground clearance needed for ripio/gravel roads in Lake District and Carretera Austral
vehicleRecommendation.trunkSpace: Adequate for 3 adults + small suitcases. Tucson/Sportage best for space.
vehicleRecommendation.note: Manual transmission SUVs are less common — book EARLY!

costEstimate.note: Total estimate for 23-day trip, 3 adults. Per person ~$625-946 USD for all transport.

ripioInfo.whatIsRipio: Gravel/dirt roads common in southern Chile, especially Lake District side roads, national parks, and Carretera Austral

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: LYS Rent a Car
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: Allows gravel roads with appropriate vehicle (SUV/crossover). Insurance covers if vehicle matches road type.

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: Europcar
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: SUV/4x4 categories permitted on unpaved roads. Standard CDW applies. Clarify at booking.

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: Chilean Rent-A-Car
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: Allows unpaved roads with SUV category. Check specific terms at booking.

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: Hertz
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: SUV/pickup categories generally permitted on maintained gravel roads. Confirm at pickup.

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: Budget/Avis
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: Mixed reports. Some vehicles restricted to paved roads only. Must confirm at booking.

companiesThatAllowRipio.name: Econorent
companiesThatAllowRipio.policy: Low ratings, unclear gravel road policy. Not recommended.

ripioInfo.insuranceWarning: CRITICAL: Many companies void insurance on gravel roads if vehicle is NOT an SUV/4x4 category. Always get written confirmation that your specific vehicle + insurance covers ripio.
tipsForRipio: Reduce speed to 40-60 km/h on gravel — stones chip windshields and undercarriage
Increase following distance — dust clouds reduce visibility
Check tire pressure daily — lower slightly (28-30 PSI) for better grip on gravel
Carry a spare tire and know how to change it
Avoid sudden braking or sharp turns — loose gravel = less traction
Watch for washboard sections (corrugations) — find the 'sweet spot' speed
Pull over for oncoming trucks — they throw rocks
Park facing downhill on steep gravel — easier to start

steps: Pull safely off the road. Turn on hazard lights.
Call rental company's 24/7 roadside assistance number (on your contract)
If no cell signal: flag down passing vehicles — Chileans are incredibly helpful
Emergency: call 133 (Carabineros) — they coordinate rescues even in remote areas
If you have a flat: change to spare tire, drive slowly to nearest town
Document everything with photos for insurance claim
emergencyKit: Spare tire + jack + wrench (verify at pickup!)
Warm clothing layers (weather changes fast in the south)
Physical map as backup to offline maps

additionalDriverInfo.howItWorks: All additional drivers MUST be registered on the rental contract at pickup
additionalDriverInfo.typicalFee: $5-10 USD per additional driver per day
additionalDriverInfo.requirements: Each driver needs: valid license + passport + must be present at pickup to sign
additionalDriverInfo.tip: Some companies include 1 additional driver free. LYS and Chilean Rent-A-Car sometimes negotiate this for longer rentals (23 days = leverage!)
additionalDriverInfo.forThreeDrivers: Primary driver: free. 2 additional drivers × $5-8/day × 23 days = $230-368 USD extra. Ask for a discount!

rentalCompanies.name: Europcar

rentalCompanies.ripioNote: SUV category allowed on unpaved roads

rentalCompanies.name: Econorent

dailyRates.note: Prices from aggregator sites, actual may vary

categories.examples: Hyundai i10, Suzuki Alto, Kia Picanto
Kia Rio, Hyundai Accent, Chevrolet Onix
Nissan Versa, VW Voyage, Citroen C-Elysee

categories.recommendedNote: Best value for ripio roads + 3 adults

categories.examples: Hyundai Tucson, Kia Sportage, Subaru Forester
categories.recommendedNote: Best trunk space for 3 adults + luggage

categories.examples: Toyota Hilux, Nissan Navara, Mitsubishi L200

fuelInfo.availability: Frequent on Ruta 5, scarcer on Carretera Austral and rural Lake District
fuelInfo.ruta5Stations: Copec stations every 50-80km on Ruta 5. Always available.
fuelInfo.lakedistrict: Fuel in all towns (Pucón, Villarrica, Osorno, etc.) but some national park roads have NO stations for 100+ km
tips: Full-service stations (attendants pump gas for you)
Credit cards accepted at most stations
Copec 'Pronto' stores have good food, coffee, clean bathrooms
Always fill up before leaving main highway
On ripio roads: fill up at EVERY opportunity — detours burn more fuel
SUV uses more fuel (~9-11L/100km) than compact (~7L/100km)

drivingRules.headlights: Must be ON at all times, even during day

required: Valid driver's license from home country — Latin alphabet readable (ALL 3 drivers)
Credit card in primary renter's name (not debit/prepaid)
recommended: International Driving Permit (IDP) — not strictly required for German/EU licenses, but recommended for all 3 drivers
Second credit card as backup for guarantee deposit
Copy of rental terms showing ripio/gravel is covered
additionalDrivers: ALL 3 drivers MUST be registered on the rental contract
Each additional driver must be present at pickup with license + passport
Additional driver fee: typically $5-10/day per extra driver
Ask for discount on 23-day rental — some companies waive or reduce additional driver fees for longer rentals
Some brokers (LYS) may include 1 free additional driver
notes: Foreign tourists can drive with home country license for up to 90 days
IDP is required if license uses non-Latin characters (Chinese, Arabic, etc.)
German/EU licenses are accepted without IDP
Credit card must have sufficient limit for guarantee deposit (CLP 500,000–2,000,000)

bookingPlatforms.name: LYS Rent a Car (direct)
bookingPlatforms.note: Best local broker, transparent pricing, ripio-friendly

relocationDeals.howItWorks: Rental companies need cars moved between cities. You drive them for FREE (0 or very low daily cost), sometimes with fuel and TAG included.
limitations: Usually 2-5 day windows to complete the drive
Seasonal: more deals during/after peak season
Vehicle type is whatever they need moved — may not be SUV
relocationDeals.tip: March is PERFECT timing — end of summer, companies need cars moved back north. Check regularly for Santiago→Puerto Montt relocations!

ruta5_santiago_to_puerto_montt.services: Regular rest stops, Copec stations, restaurants

lake_district_side_roads.quality: Mixed — paved main roads + gravel/ripio side roads
lake_district_side_roads.surface: Asphalt on main routes, gravel on roads to volcanoes, hot springs, national parks
lake_district_side_roads.services: Available in towns, sparse in parks
lake_district_side_roads.recommendation: Compact SUV minimum for side roads and national park access

carretera_austral.recommendation: SUV or pickup truck strongly recommended
